reasoning: PTPN2 (TC‑PTP) is a tyrosine phosphatase that attenuates cytokine‑ and hormone‑induced JAK/STAT signaling, including leptin‑STAT3 pathways in the hypothalamus. When PTPN2 is reduced, STAT3 remains more phosphorylated and active. In hypothalamic neurons, activated STAT3 represses transcription of the orexigenic neuropeptide Y (NPY) gene. Therefore, loss of PTPN2 is expected to enhance leptin‑STAT3 signaling, leading to decreased Npy expression.
